title: Decision: run_on_new_thread / scoped-thread init pattern must not be simplified
description: secretx-core run_on_new_thread() and get_blocking() spawn a scoped OS thread with its own tokio current-thread runtime for blocking initialization. A contributor will suggest replacing this with tokio::task::block_in_place() or Handle::block_on() which look simpler. Both alternatives panic when called from a current_thread runtime (the default in #[tokio::test] and common in single-threaded services). The scoped-thread pattern works in every runtime configuration because the new thread owns its own runtime. The comment in secretx-aws-ssm::build_ssm_client() already states this explicitly; the same rationale applies to all backends that use run_on_new_thread.
